task_completion.description: Three simulated device adapters are created in OpenICE
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenICE Supervisor or Device Adapter interface shows the active status of a 'Multiparameter Monitor', a 'Pulse Oximeter', and a Temperature-capable device.

task_completion.description: The 'Vital Signs' clinical application is open and confirming data flow
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE 'Vital Signs' application window is visible, displaying live physiological data (e.g., waveforms, numeric values for HR, SpO2, Temp) from the connected simulated devices.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the active setup is saved to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'mews_monitoring_screenshot.png' is visible on the Desktop, or a screenshot tool/terminal command is seen saving the image to '/home/ga/Desktop/mews_monitoring_screenshot.png'.

task_completion.description: The feasibility assessment text file is created on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal is open showing the creation or editing of a file named 'mews_feasibility_assessment.txt' at the Desktop path, or the file icon is visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The assessment includes the list of devices and a MEWS scoring table
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the text file show a list of the three created devices (Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter, Temperature) and a reference table or structured list of MEWS scoring ranges.

task_completion.description: The assessment includes the Automation Gap Analysis identifying the non-automatable parameter and a final recommendation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file explicitly contains an 'Automation Gap Analysis' section that identifies 'AVPU' or 'consciousness' as the parameter requiring manual assessment/cannot be automated by sensors, along with a final recommendation section.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually used the OpenICE GUI to configure the devices and view vital signs
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the OpenICE Supervisor/Device Adapter menus and Vital Signs app, rather than just displaying a static image or faking the setup.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actively took a screenshot of their own setup
integrity.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool (like xfce4-screenshooter) or terminal command (like scrot/import) is visible capturing the active OpenICE setup.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent generated the text file content themselves
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file is seen being typed out in a text editor or generated via echo/cat commands in the terminal, rather than copying or moving a pre-existing completed file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: MEWS (Modified Early Warning Score) consists of 5 parameters: Heart rate, Systolic blood pressure, Respiratory rate, Temperature, and Level of consciousness (AVPU). The 'Automation Gap Analysis' must identify that AVPU (Alert, Voice, Pain, Unresponsive) or 'consciousness' is the only parameter that requires manual clinical assessment and cannot be automated by standard medical sensors. OpenICE supports simulated devices like Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter, and Temperature devices, which can automate the other 4 parameters.
